name: ai-attack-hardening
description: Harden verify and join flows against AI agents, automation, keystroke replay, and bot farms
goals: Server-verifiable verify sessions; no raw API submit bypass
Automation and synthetic keystroke detection with channel policy
Precheck oracle closed; join burst signals for farm waves
Regression tests for every new control

requirements.description: Verify session binding: precheck issues challenge secret; submit requires valid proof
Per-token precheck rate limit and first visitor_id bind on token row
acceptance: Submit with mismatched visitor_id vs first precheck forces pending or 400 per policy

requirements.description: Keystroke plausibility validation and payload bounds on submit
acceptance: SubmitBody enforces max keystrokes length and body size
Flat/synthetic rhythm flagged synthetic_keystroke in risk_factors
tests cover too-fast and too-uniform keystroke sessions

requirements.description: Precheck oracle reduction and trusted proxy IP handling
acceptance: ip_flagged not returned to verify client JSON

requirements.description: Extended env_flags collection and composite automation score in matching
acceptance: matching.py computes automation contribution to pending decision
tests/test_matching.py covers webdriver pending under strict automation mode

requirements.description: Security wizard v4 step for automation strictness
Join burst score wired into verify matching and ops logging
acceptance: High join velocity adds risk factor at verify submit
log_to_ops_channel includes burst context when applicable
tests cover burst factor in check_known_bad

requirements.description: AI hardening operator docs and audit report
acceptance: DEPLOY.md documents new env vars and strict preset behavior
